high.mackenzie.snowflake

Class ParserOutput

  • java.lang.Object
    • high.mackenzie.snowflake.ParserOutput

  • public final class ParserOutput
extends java.lang.Object
    An instance of this class is the output of a parser.

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      int lengthOfConsumption()
      This method returns the farthest location that parsing reached.
      ITreeNode parseTree()
      This method retrieves and returns the parse-tree created by this parser.
      void print(java.io.PrintStream stream, boolean estimate, boolean exact, boolean trace)
      Equivalent to: print(stream, NewlineStyles.fromSystem(), estimate, exact, trace).
      void print(java.io.PrintStream stream, NewlineStyles newline, boolean estimate, boolean exact, boolean trace)
      Convenience overload of: print(PrintWriter, boolean, boolean, boolean).
      void print(java.io.PrintWriter writer, boolean estimate, boolean exact, boolean trace)
      Equivalent to: print(writer, NewlineStyles.fromSystem(), estimate, exact, trace).
      void print(java.io.PrintWriter writer, NewlineStyles newline, boolean estimate, boolean exact, boolean trace)
      This method prints a message describing the status of the output.
      void requireSuccess()
      This method makes reporting the existence of syntax errors simpler.
      boolean success()
      This method determines whether parsing succeeded.
      Trace trace()
      This method returns a record describing stages of the parsing-attempt.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Method Detail

      • success

        public boolean success()
        This method determines whether parsing succeeded.
        Returns:
        true, if and only if, parsing was successful.

      • lengthOfConsumption

        public int lengthOfConsumption()
        This method returns the farthest location that parsing reached.

        The returned position is often useful, when searching for syntax-errors.

        Returns:
        the aforedescribed location, which is the index relating to the parser's input.

      • trace

        public Trace trace()
        This method returns a record describing stages of the parsing-attempt.

        The returned list may be useful for debugging grammar and/or finding syntax-errors.

        Returns:
        the aforedescribed record.

      • parseTree

        public ITreeNode parseTree()
        This method retrieves and returns the parse-tree created by this parser.

        The returned value is actually the root node in the parse-tree.

        Returns:
        the parse-tree created by the parser, or null, if no such parse-tree exists.

      • print

        public void print(java.io.PrintWriter writer,
         NewlineStyles newline,
         boolean estimate,
         boolean exact,
         boolean trace)
        This method prints a message describing the status of the output.

        If sucess() == true, then the string "Parsing Succeeded!" is printed.

        If sucess() == false, then the string "Parsing Failed!" is printed.

        Parameters:
        writer - is the writer to write to.
        newline - is the style of the newlines in the parser's input.
        estimate - is true, if and only if the line and column numbers are to be written also.
        exact - is true, if and only if the length of consumption is to be written also.
        trace - is true, if and only if the trace is to be written also.

      • print

        public void print(java.io.PrintStream stream,
         NewlineStyles newline,
         boolean estimate,
         boolean exact,
         boolean trace)
        Convenience overload of: print(PrintWriter, boolean, boolean, boolean).

        See the aforementioned method for more details.

        Parameters:
        stream - is the stream to write to.
        newline - is the style of the newlines in the parser's input.
        estimate - is true, if and only if the line and column numbers are to be written.
        exact - is true, if and only if the length of consumption is to be written.
        trace - is true, if and only if the trace is to be written.

      • print

        public void print(java.io.PrintWriter writer,
         boolean estimate,
         boolean exact,
         boolean trace)
        Equivalent to: print(writer, NewlineStyles.fromSystem(), estimate, exact, trace).

      • print

        public void print(java.io.PrintStream stream,
         boolean estimate,
         boolean exact,
         boolean trace)
        Equivalent to: print(stream, NewlineStyles.fromSystem(), estimate, exact, trace).

      • requireSuccess

        public void requireSuccess()
                    throws ParsingFailedException
        This method makes reporting the existence of syntax errors simpler.
        Throws:
        ParsingFailedException - if(!this.success())
